experiment.description: Generate a family of 30 synthetic domains varying in compressibility (n_comp vs n_incomp groups, group sizes). For each domain and each DL mode (clauses, bits), dream symbolically then add new entities and measure recovery of ground-truth derived facts. Compressible groups (size >= 3) always recover (rule discovered); incompressible groups never recover (no rule). Recovery = n_comp/(n_comp+n_incomp). Compute Spearman and Pearson correlations between compression metrics (bits saved, bits ratio, clauses saved, clause ratio) and recovery across the domain family. Tests whether compression predicts generalization and whether bits-saved is the better predictor.
experiment.name: does compression predict generalization

seeds.note: fully deterministic; domain structure fixed by spec
